etymology.description: 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") + 先(先) (xiān) = activity involving water -> wash
etymology.interpretation: The character 洗(xǐ, "wash") combines 水(水) (shuǐ, "water"), which signifies the action involving water, with 先(先) (xiān) providing the phonetic component. This combination effectively conveys the concept of washing or cleansing, which inherently involves water.

examples.chinese: 我每天洗衣服(我每天洗衣服)。
请在饭前洗手(請在飯前洗手)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng zài fàn qián xǐ shǒu.
examples.english: Please wash your hands before eating.

examples.chinese: 她喜欢用温水洗脸(她喜歡用溫水洗臉)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān yòng wēnshuǐ xǐ liǎn.
examples.english: She likes to wash her face with warm water.
